title: Leader Failure Divergence Trap
focus_questions: Has the original leader already failed while the lagger still prices the old story?
Are lagger followers now trapped by leader failure?

summary.thesis: A divergence trap appears when the market that led first fails its own hold, while the lagging market is still being traded as if the original story remains valid.
summary.operator_translation: Do not keep trading the lagging market in the old direction once the leader has clearly failed.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when one market led the move, then lost hold quality while the related market still looks late and extended in the old direction.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: cross-symbol move with one market clearly leading first. Required evidence: the leader loses hold quality, the lagger is still late in the old direction, and divergence becomes visible. Do not trade: by clinging to the lagger's late follow-through after the leader has failed. Real trigger: confirmed leader failure plus lagger non-confirmation or reversal. Failure point: the leader never actually loses hold quality or quickly regains it.

when_relevant: One market led the move or break first.
The other market followed late or emotionally.
The leader then starts failing while the lagger still prices the old move.
required_evidence: the leader loses hold quality or fails its break
the lagging market remains late, extended, or falsely confirmatory while divergence appears
confirmation_signals: the leader first appears valid, then loses hold quality or fails its break
the lagging market continues to price the old story longer than the leader
once divergence becomes obvious, the lagging market reverses or releases trapped followers
invalidation_signals: the leader never actually fails its hold quality
the lagger resets quickly and synchronizes again without trapping followers
divergence is being called before the leader's failure is visible
no_trade_conditions: Do not keep trading the lagging market in the old direction once the leader has clearly failed.
Do not call divergence actionable before the leader's failure is actually visible.
Do not fade the lagger too early if the leader has not yet lost its hold quality.

entry_archetypes.name: leader-failure lagger unwind
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the lagger begins unwinding the old move once the leader has already failed.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the leader failure is still ambiguous.

entry_archetypes.name: late-follow trap release
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the lagger proves its late confirmation was invalid and starts releasing trapped followers.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the lagger has already fully reset and the trap edge is gone.

management_notes: This card is strongest after a prior leader already signaled the move and then failed.
The edge is usually in the lagger unwind, not in the first tiny divergence tick.
Once the lagger fully resets and synchronizes again, the divergence trap is mostly over.
review_questions: Did the leader really fail, or was the read simply early?
Was the lagging market still being traded as if confirmation remained valid?
When divergence appeared, did the operator react to the leader failure or cling to the late follow-through story?
